# {{SHOW_NAME}} — Series Bible

_Show id: `{{SHOW_ID}}`. Created {{DATE}}._
_(Seeded from the new-show template — replace every [BRACKETED] prompt with real content.)_

> ⚠️ **Doc isolation:** MACU Studio's Canon Docs panel lists `docs/_common/` (shared pipeline docs, tagged
> SHARED) plus this show's `docs/shows/{{SHOW_ID}}/` files. Editing a SHARED doc changes it for every show;
> editing these per-show files only touches {{SHOW_NAME}}. Keep show content here, never in the `_common`
> or another show's files.

---

## House style / shared universe (set the context)

[FILL: the shared world, tone, and visual register this show lives in — the context every episode inherits.
 If this show belongs to a larger universe of shows, describe what they all share here, then specialize
 below. The studio's built-in look is a black-and-white 1970s analog-TV aesthetic; keep it, specialize it,
 or replace it. e.g. "A deadpan, post-apocalyptic, retro-futurist world rendered as grainy 1970s analog TV;
 the facts of the world are insane and stated as plain fact — never explained, never winked at."]

## Logline

[ONE OR TWO SENTENCES: what is this show, who's in it, what's the format (newscast / soap / game show /
ad / cooking show…), and what's the joke engine. e.g. "A bone-dry daytime soap about catastrophically
privileged people in a sealed bunker conducting overwrought melodrama over a profoundly stupid fortune."]

**Tagline:** _[A single melodramatic or deadpan line that captures the show. Optional but recommended.]_

## The One Rule (tone law)

[FILL: the single rule that governs every line of this show — the tonal law future writers must never
break. What's the specific flavor here? What does the cast take deathly seriously that the audience finds
absurd? What is never acknowledged? e.g. "Bone dry. It takes itself to death — the cast never laughs; the
audience does all the laughing."]

## Setting

[WHERE does this show live — the single anchor location the cast orbits (a desk, a bunker, a kitchen, a
shop). Soaps/sitcoms need ONE recurring place. Describe it, and the askew-but-unacknowledged detail that
makes it feel off. e.g. a lakeside community with no lake; a newsroom broadcasting to no one.]

## The economy / the absurd fact

[Deadpan shows often rest on one STUPID THING treated as sacred — the resource, the stakes, the MacGuffin.
What is it here, and what's its straight-faced origin? e.g. "the last drink powder on Earth, from a beached
container barge." Keep it understated and load-bearing.]

## Core premise / what generates episodes

[What's the repeatable engine that makes new episodes? The closed-economy conflict, the weekly format, the
recurring problem. A show without an engine runs out after two episodes.]

## Signature running gag(s)

[The show's load-bearing recurring bit(s). Describe the mechanic precisely and how it escalates — future
agents must run it consistently. Track its state in Story_Arcs.md. e.g. a character who keeps dying and
returning in a downgraded form each week.]
